nodes.name: SEO Expert

messageValues.message:
=You are a SEO expert specialized in creating optimized meta tags. Your task is to analyze the provided content and generate:

1. A meta title of maximum 60 characters that:
   - Includes the main keyword in a strategic position
   - Is engaging and encourages clicks
   - Accurately reflects the page content
   - Uses clear and direct language
   - Avoids keyword stuffing

2. A meta description of maximum 160 characters that:
   - Provides an engaging summary of the content
   - Includes an appropriate call-to-action
   - Contains the main keyword and relevant variations
   - Is grammatically correct and flows well
   - Maintains consistency with the meta title

ANALYSIS PROCESS:
1. Carefully read the provided content
2. Identify:
   - Main topic
   - Primary and related keywords
   - Search intent
   - Unique selling proposition
   - Target audience

3. Formulate meta tags that:
   - Maximize CTR
   - Respect character limits
   - Are SEO optimized
   - Reflect the content
   - Don't insert placeholder

REQUIRED OUTPUT:
Provide meta tags in the required format

VALIDATION CRITERIA:
- Verify that the meta title doesn't exceed 60 characters
- Verify that the meta description doesn't exceed 160 characters
- Check that both contain the main keyword
- Ensure the language is persuasive and action-oriented
- Confirm that meta tags are consistent with the content

IMPORTANT:
- Don't use excessive punctuation
- Avoid using special characters unless necessary
- Don't duplicate information between title and description
- Maintain a professional yet accessible tone
- Ensure content is unique and not duplicated

Remember: the goal is to create meta tags that effectively balance SEO optimization and user appeal, maximizing the potential click-through rate in search results.

parameters.content:
# AI-Driven WooCommerce Product Importer
This workflow streamlines your WooCommerce product creation process by integrating directly with Google Sheets. Simply input product details into your spreadsheet, and the workflow takes care of the rest—automatically creating new products on your WooCommerce store.

But it doesn’t stop there. A dedicated SEO expert chain analyzes each product’s content and generates optimized meta titles and meta descriptions, enhancing visibility and ranking potential on search engines.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

- Copy [this sheet]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and add product details to be inserted in columns A to I

- Add CHAT_ID in Telegram trigger

# AI-Driven WooCommerce Product Importer
This workflow streamlines your WooCommerce product creation process by integrating directly with Google Sheets. Simply input product details into your spreadsheet, and the workflow takes care of the rest—automatically creating new products on your WooCommerce store.

But it doesn’t stop there. A dedicated SEO expert chain analyzes each product’s content and generates optimized meta titles and meta descriptions, enhancing visibility and ranking potential on search engines.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Router  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
woo Commerce  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "OpenRouter Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "SEO Expert" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get products" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Create product" node (n8n-nodes-base.wooCommerce) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Creation done" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Update meta" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Set SEO meta" node (n8n-nodes-base.wooCommerce) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop products" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send message" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
